elements.section-2411-002: Brief introduction to Sasaki Geometry and the Boothby-Wang Construction\label{sasakibackground}
elements.sent-2411-066: Sasaki geometry can be thought of as the odd dimensional sister geometry of K\"ahler geometry.
elements.sent-2411-067: Indeed, up to an infinitesimal deformation, a Sasakian structure is an $S^1$ orbibundle over a K\"ahler orbifold.
elements.sent-2411-068: This is the orbifold version of a construction due originally to Boothby and Wang \cite{BoWa58 in the context of contact and symplectic geometries.}
elements.sent-2411-069: When the Reeb vector field of the contact structure has closed orbits the symplectic base is an orbifold, and when the symplectic base has a K\"ahlerian structure the total space of the $S^1$ orbibundle has a Sasakian structure.
elements.sent-2411-070: We refer to this construction as the orbifold Boothby-Wang construction, or just the Boothby-Wang construction for short when the context is clear.
elements.sent-2411-071: It is important to realize that there are Sasakian structures which do not have a well-defined base.
elements.sent-2411-072: These arise when the orbits of the Reeb vector field are not closed, but their closure generates a higher dimensional torus.
elements.sent-2411-073: To summarize, Sasaki geometry can be understood, up to infinitesimal deformations, in terms of the bundle structure \centerline{circle $\lra$ Sasaki $\lra$ K\"ahler.}
elements.sent-2411-074: For more details see Section 7.1 in \cite{BG05 .}
elements.sent-2411-075: In this paper, whenever we are considering a Sasaki structure $(\cald, J, \xi)$ with Sasaki metric $g$ we will use $Scal(g)$ to denote the Tanaka-Webster scalar curvature $Scal(\xi)$ of $\xi$.

elements.section-2411-006: Twins of Toric extremal K\"ahler metrics on quadrilaterals

elements.sent-2411-363: In other words, our viewpoint will be to start with an actual extremal K\"ahler metric $g$ and then look for a non-constant Killing potential $f>0$ such that $(g,1)$ and $(g,f)$ are $4$-weighted extremal K\"ahler twins.

elements.sent-2411-365: From \cite{Leg09 we know that if $g$ is csc then $g$ is either Calabi toric, orthotoric, or a product metric.}
elements.sent-2411-366: For this reason, and given our particular interest in csc structures, we will focus this section on those three subcases of toric K\"ahler structures on quadrilaterals.
